name: Gnocchi w/ Porcini Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Bake the potatoes until tender. While hot, cut them in half and scoop out the pulp.
2. Work the pulp through a ricer into a large bowl (or mash using an up-and-down motion only).
3. Using a fork, stir in the eggs.
4. Blend in 1/2 cup flour. Turn the dough onto a work surface.
5. Gradually knead in 2 cups of flour. Continue kneading until smooth and elastic, about 10 minutes.
6. Shape the dough into a 6x4-inch loaf.
7. Cut the loaf into 1-inch strips.
8. Roll the strips into cylinders that are 1/2 inch in diameter.
9. Cut the cylinders into 1 1/4-inch pieces.
10. Boil one piece for 2 to 3 minutes. If it falls apart, add more flour to the dough a quarter cup at a time until the pieces hold together when boiled.
11. Using a floured fork, press the tines downward into each piece so it curls around the fork.
12. Arrange the gnocchi in a single layer without touching on a generously floured towel.
13. Let the gnocchi stand for 3 to 4 hours at room temperature to dry.
14. Bring water and salt to boil.
15. Add the gnocchi.
16. Stir gently with a wooden spoon.
17. When the gnocchi comes to the surface, let cook for 2 minutes.
18. Drain well.
19. Place on a platter.
20. Spoon Tomato-Porcini Sauce over.
21. Dot with butter.
22. Sprinkle with Parmesan.
